openmeter/billing/worker/advance.purpose: Batch auto-advance loop for StandardInvoices: lists invoices in DraftWaitingAutoApproval, DraftWaitingForCollection, and stuck-advanceable states, then fans out parallel goroutines (batched) to call billing.Service.AdvanceInvoice. Acts as the scheduled/polling counterpart to the event-driven asyncadvance handler.

patterns.name: Config struct constructor with validation
patterns.description: NewAdvancer accepts a Config struct; returns error if BillingService or Logger is nil. All new workers in this package must follow this pattern.
patterns.example: func NewAdvancer(config Config) (*AutoAdvancer, error) { if config.BillingService == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf(...) } }

patterns.name: Batched parallel fan-out with errChan + sync.WaitGroup
patterns.description: lo.Chunk splits work into batches; each batch spawns goroutines writing to a buffered errChan. sync.OnceFunc closes the channel after all batches complete. errors.Join collects all failures.
patterns.example: errChan := make(chan error, len(invoices)); closeErrChan := sync.OnceFunc(func() { close(errChan) }); defer closeErrChan()

patterns.name: ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance is a non-fatal sentinel
patterns.description: When AdvanceInvoice returns billing.ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance, log a warning with invoice details and return nil — do not propagate as an error.
patterns.example: if errors.Is(err, billing.ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance) { a.logger.WarnContext(ctx, ...) ; return invoice, nil }

patterns.name: List consolidation with lo.UniqBy
patterns.description: ListInvoicesToAdvance merges three separate list calls and deduplicates by invoice ID using lo.UniqBy before batching.
patterns.example: return lo.UniqBy(allInvoices, func(i billing.StandardInvoice) string { return i.ID }), nil

patterns.name: Context with cancel for batch runs
patterns.description: All() immediately creates a cancellable child context so in-flight goroutines can be cancelled if the parent is cancelled.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package — AutoAdvancer struct with All(), ListInvoicesToAdvance(), ListInvoicesPendingAutoAdvance(), ListInvoicesPendingCollection(), ListStuckInvoicesNeedingAdvance(), AdvanceInvoice(), and NewAdvancer().
key_file_guides.watch_for: batchSize=0 means single batch (all invoices in one pass). errChan capacity must equal len(invoices), not len(batches), or sends will block. ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance must remain a warning, not an error.

anti_patterns: Calling billing.Adapter directly — all DB access must go through billing.Service
Propagating billing.ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance as an error return from AdvanceInvoice
Forgetting to deduplicate the merged invoice list before batching, causing double-advances
Using context.Background() instead of the caller-supplied ctx in goroutines

decisions.decision: Three separate list queries merged with deduplication instead of a single compound query
decisions.rationale: Waiting-auto-approval, waiting-for-collection, and stuck invoices have distinct filter shapes in billing.ListStandardInvoicesInput; merging at the query level would require OR semantics the adapter does not support cleanly.

decisions.decision: Batched parallel goroutines rather than sequential advance
decisions.rationale: Invoice advancement is per-invoice and independent; parallelism is safe and reduces wall-clock time for large namespaces.

code_examples.scenario: Advance all eligible invoices in batches of 50

openmeter/billing/worker/asyncadvance.purpose: Watermill event handler for async invoice advancement: receives a billing.AdvanceStandardInvoiceEvent from the system Kafka topic and calls billing.Service.AdvanceInvoice once, treating ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance as a benign late-message condition. Requires billing service configured with ForegroundAdvancementStrategy to prevent infinite retry loops.

patterns.name: Config.Validate() guards strategy mismatch
patterns.description: New() calls c.Validate() which rejects any BillingService not using ForegroundAdvancementStrategy — prevents infinite event loops where background advancement re-emits the same advance event.
patterns.example: if c.BillingService.GetAdvancementStrategy() != billing.ForegroundAdvancementStrategy { return errors.New("billing service must have foreground advancement strategy...") }

patterns.name: Handle() has a single-event signature
patterns.description: Handler.Handle(ctx, *billing.AdvanceStandardInvoiceEvent) is the only exported method beyond New() — matches the Watermill NoPublisherHandler closure signature expected by the worker router.

patterns.name: ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance → warn + nil return
patterns.description: Late Kafka messages for already-advanced invoices must log a warning and return nil (no requeue); all other errors propagate normally.
patterns.example: if errors.Is(err, billing.ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance) { h.logger.WarnContext(...); return nil }

key_file_guides.role: Entire package — Config, Config.Validate(), New(), Handler.Handle(). No other files.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Forgetting the strategy validation in Validate() creates an infinite loop: background strategy re-emits advance events, which this handler re-processes forever.

anti_patterns: Wiring this handler with a BillingService that has BackgroundAdvancementStrategy — Validate() will catch it at startup, not at runtime
Returning ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance from Handle() — it will cause Watermill to nack and potentially requeue
Adding state to Handler — the struct is intentionally stateless beyond service + logger

decisions.decision: Strategy guard in Config.Validate() rather than a runtime check inside Handle()
decisions.rationale: Misconfiguration (background strategy + this handler) creates an infinite loop that is hard to detect at runtime; failing fast at construction time is safer.

openmeter/billing/worker/collect.purpose: Batch invoice collection loop: lists GatheringInvoices whose NextCollectionAt is due, deduplicates by customer, and fans out parallel CollectCustomerInvoice calls that trigger billing.Service.InvoicePendingLines. Handles namespace locking, empty-line recalculation, and locked namespace exclusion.

patterns.name: Config struct constructor with nil checks
patterns.description: NewInvoiceCollector validates GatheringInvoiceService, BillingService, and Logger are non-nil before constructing the struct.

patterns.name: Input types with Validate() method
patterns.description: ListCollectableInvoicesInput and CollectCustomerInvoiceInput each carry a Validate() error method checked at the top of their callers.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid input: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Sentinel errors drive recovery, not failure
patterns.description: ErrNamespaceLocked → warn + return nil. ErrInvoiceCreateNoLines → warn + RecalculateGatheringInvoices + return nil. Only unknown errors propagate.
patterns.example: if errors.Is(err, billing.ErrNamespaceLocked) { a.logger.WarnContext(...); return nil, nil }

patterns.name: Collection uses WithPartialInvoiceLinesDisabled option
patterns.description: System-initiated collection must always call InvoicePendingLines with billing.WithPartialInvoiceLinesDisabled() to disable progressive billing.

patterns.name: Locked namespace exclusion before fan-out
patterns.description: All() filters customer IDs whose namespace appears in a.lockedNamespaces (slices.Contains) before building batches — prevents wasted goroutine launches.
patterns.example: customerIDs = lo.Filter(..., func(id customer.CustomerID, _ int) bool { return !slices.Contains(a.lockedNamespaces, id.Namespace) })

patterns.name: Batched parallel fan-out with errChan + sync.WaitGroup
patterns.description: Same pattern as advance package: lo.Chunk, goroutines per batch item, buffered errChan sized to total customer count, sync.OnceFunc closer, errors.Join.
patterns.example: errChan := make(chan error, len(customerIDs)); closeErrChan := sync.OnceFunc(func() { close(errChan) }); defer closeErrChan()

key_file_guides.role: Entire package — InvoiceCollector with All(), ListCollectableInvoices(), CollectCustomerInvoice(), and NewInvoiceCollector(). GatheringInvoiceService and BillingService are held as separate fields for targeted use.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ListCollectableInvoices uses a filter.FilterTime OR clause to include invoices with nil NextCollectionAt — removing the nil branch would silently skip legacy invoices. errChan must be sized to len(customerIDs) not len(batches).

anti_patterns: Calling InvoicePendingLines without billing.WithPartialInvoiceLinesDisabled() — allows progressive billing in system-initiated collection, which is explicitly forbidden
Treating ErrNamespaceLocked or ErrInvoiceCreateNoLines as hard errors — both have defined recovery paths (skip and recalculate respectively)
Skipping the locked namespace filter before fan-out — causes redundant errors for every customer in a locked namespace
Using context.Background() inside goroutines instead of the parent ctx
Forgetting lo.Uniq on customerIDs before batching — a customer with multiple gathering invoices would be collected twice

decisions.decision: Collect at customer granularity, not invoice granularity
decisions.rationale: InvoicePendingLines operates per-customer and may merge multiple pending lines into one invoice; iterating by invoice ID would cause double-collect races.

decisions.decision: ErrInvoiceCreateNoLines triggers RecalculateGatheringInvoices instead of failing
decisions.rationale: This state indicates stale gathering invoice state (possible data inconsistency); a recalculation heals it without surfacing an error to the caller.

code_examples.scenario: Collect all due invoices for a namespace in batches of 100
